notes: Virginia launched a dedicated RHTP website at ruralhealthtransformationva.virginia.gov (separate from the DMAS page). Four initiatives: CareIQ ($280M+ in health IT over 5 years), Homegrown Health Heroes (residencies), Connected Care Closer to Home (mobile clinics, telehealth), Live Well Together (wearables, food-as-medicine). Six rural regions: Southwest, Roanoke/Alleghany, Southside, Northern Piedmont, Shenandoah Valley, Eastern Shore/Northern Neck. Year 1 pilots launch in 'test and learn' approach; Years 2-3 competitive grants. Per new site's Application & Award page (as of June 2026): Virginia has NOT yet opened formal Requests for Applications for RHT funding to external applicants. Implementation will proceed via Key Implementation Partners leading sub-initiatives. Virginia held a public webinar June 3, 2026 (2-3pm) titled 'Rural Health Transformation in Virginia: Updates, Opportunities, and Next Steps' - registration was full. Newsletter signup available on new site for future funding opportunity alerts. Monitor: DMAS procurement and eVA portal (eva.virginia.gov - search 'Medical' under Buying Entity). Implementation partners: Virginia Hospital & Healthcare Association (VHHA), Virginia Center for Health Innovation (VCHI - confirmed as independent evaluator), UnitedHealthcare Community & State. General Assembly amendment directs $10.0M annually from RHT Initiative to Connected Care Closer to Home via rural free clinics ($10M includes telehealth-enabled services). Budget period spending deadlines range from 9/30/2026 through 9/30/2032.

notes: HB 122 signed by Gov. Gordon March 6, 2026. CMS approved Year 1 plan mid-May 2026 ($205M); perpetuity fund NOT approved. Per WDH program page (as of June 2026), tentative schedule: June-July 2026 - RFA/RFP development; July 2026 - tentative application submission window; Aug-Sept 2026 - awards made and funds drawn down. State must obligate Year 1 funds by Oct 31, 2026. Four funding initiatives: (1) Access to Emergency Care; (2) Health Technology Transformation; (3) Rural Workforce Supply; (4) Make Wyoming Healthy Again. Time-limited funds will be distributed through competitive RFPs/RFAs managed by WDH. Per Wyoming Medical Society, allocation categories include: Integrated Primary Care/FQHC Expansion (36.4%), Technology Adoption (15.1%), Care Coordination (8.2%), Tele-Specialist Platform (8.2%), Workforce Education Startup Costs (8.2%), Centralized Billing Capacity for EMS (4.9%), Non-Emergency Transportation Coordination (2%). WDH maintains a public document library (project/budget narratives, award notice, public engagement docs) on Google Drive linked from the program page. No specific webinar/town hall dates posted for vendors yet; informational webinars and Q&A expected to precede RFA postings.
